claim: Hand A's paragraph-structure signature (vocabulary disjunction, top-20 medial recipe-verb properties, header recurrence, anti-alternating adjacency) falls within tolerance bands of an established medieval Latin herbal-encyclopedic text (PRIMARY: Isidore of Seville, Etymologiae Book 17 'De rebus rusticis' — Latin prose, medieval-canonical, one-topic-per-section structure). If Hand A's four measured values each fall within the specified tolerance distance of Isidore's corresponding values, Hand A's signature is CONSISTENT WITH the herbal-encyclopedic genre. A secondary VERSE corpus (Macer Floridus) runs alongside as a genre-form sensitivity check: measures where Isidore and Macer agree can be trusted as genre signals; measures where they disagree by more than 2x the tolerance are flagged as form-sensitive (may reflect prose-vs-verse rather than encyclopedic-structure).
evidence_for: H-BV-RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 MARGINAL (2/3) resolved substantively to 'herbal-encyclopedic' — unique headers + reusable body + no header recurrence

Isidore of Seville's Etymologiae Book 17 is the canonical medieval Latin encyclopedic reference (compiled c.630, widely copied through 15th c.), with per-topic prose entries exactly matching the encyclopedic structure we are testing for
A dual-benchmark design (prose primary + verse secondary) guards against both threshold-calibration errors and genre-form confounds
evidence_against: Isidore's sections are subdivided [1], [2], ... within thematic chapters ('De agricultura', 'De frumentis', etc.) — unit sizes may differ from Hand A's gallows-delimited paragraphs (mean 40 tokens)
Unit-size mismatch may affect per-paragraph type/token behaviour and header recurrence statistics; relative thresholds mitigate but do not eliminate this

A pre-registered relative-match rule with +/-5pp tolerance is stricter than an abstract threshold and risks a false negative if Isidore's paragraph structure differs from Hand A by even modest amounts that are NOT due to cipher/code structure
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — AMENDED BEFORE EXECUTION.

  CORPORA (frozen at pre-registration):
    PRIMARY (prose): Isidore of Seville, Etymologiae Book 17 'De rebus rusticis', from thelatinlibrary.com/isidore/17.shtml, saved verbatim to `raw/corpus/reference-corpora/isidore_etym_17_raw.html`. ~10,362 word tokens — padded with Etymologiae Book 18 from the same source if under 11,022.
    SECONDARY (verse, sanity check): Macer Floridus De viribus herbarum (Choulant 1832 Leipzig edition) from archive.org/details/deviribusherbaru00mace, saved to `raw/corpus/reference-corpora/macer_floridus_raw.txt`. Known verse/metre caveat — reported but NOT binding on verdict.

  PARAGRAPH SEGMENTATION (locked per corpus):
    Hand A: 271 paragraphs from H-BV-HIGH-LOW-STRUCTURE-01 (boundary = line beginning with plain gallows t or p not followed by h).
    Isidore: boundaries at each numbered section marker [N] within the Etymologiae Book 17 text (standard editorial numbering preserved in the Latin Library copy). A paragraph = content between two such markers.
    Macer: boundaries at chapter heading lines (roman numeral + period + uppercase plant name, e.g. 'III. ABSINTHIUM.'). A paragraph = verse content between two such headings, with OCR apparatus lines filtered (Greek characters, digit-initial apparatus).

  CLEANING (locked):
    Isidore: strip HTML; lowercase; replace punctuation with spaces; keep only ASCII letters and whitespace; truncate to 11,022 tokens (padding with Book 18 if needed).
    Macer: restrict to range between the first 'III. ABSINTHIUM.' line and the first 'CONFECTBUCH' line; strip Greek-containing and digit-initial lines; lowercase; keep only ASCII letters; truncate to 11,022 tokens.
    Hand A: already tokenised in H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-01.

  MEASURES (identical for all three corpora):
    (M1) HIGH/LOW split: rank R at cumulative 50% tokens.
    (M2) Vocabulary disjunction: |INITIAL_types \ MIDDLE_types| / |INITIAL_types| with position bins INITIAL/FIRST/MIDDLE/FINAL computed per paragraph.
    (M3) Top-20 MIDDLE token recipe-verb passes: consonant-skeleton variants >=2 AND paragraph-level coverage >=30%.
    (M4) Header recurrence rate: fraction of distinct INITIAL-position types that initiate >=3 paragraphs.
    (M5) Cross-class adjacency rate HL+LH using the corpus's own HIGH set.

  RELATIVE-MATCH TOLERANCE BANDS (locked — anchored to ISIDORE as the genre reference):
    The test asks: 'Does Hand A's signature fall within tolerance of an established herbal-encyclopedic text?' ISIDORE is the genre reference; Hand A is the test subject.
    Tolerance sizes (|Hand A − Isidore| thresholds, absolute distance):
      disjunction:       +/- 0.10
      top-20 medial:     +/- 4 (of 20)
      header recurrence: +/- 0.05
      cross-class rate:  +/- 0.05
    Each tolerance band is centred on Isidore's observed value at runtime. Hand A's value on each measure is then tested for inclusion in that band. A measure PASSES if |Hand A − Isidore| <= tolerance for that measure.

  GENRE-FORM SENSITIVITY FLAGGING (locked):
    For each measure, also check |Isidore − Macer|. If this inter-reference distance exceeds 2x the tolerance for that measure, the measure is FORM-SENSITIVE (prose-vs-verse difference dominates within-genre signal). Form-sensitive measures are reported separately and annotated in the verdict, but they still count in the 4-of-4 decision — this preserves the locked rule. If 3 of 4 measures are form-sensitive, add an explicit CAVEAT that the genre reference itself is unstable and the test is under-powered.

  PARAGRAPH-SIZE UNIT NORMALISATION:
    Report mean paragraph length in tokens for each corpus. If Isidore's mean paragraph length is more than 2x or less than 0.5x Hand A's 40.7 tokens, add a unit-size caveat to the verdict — the paragraphs are not size-matched and any signature deviation may reflect unit scale rather than structural genre.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED (Hand A is CONSISTENT WITH herbal-encyclopedic) if Hand A's values on ALL FOUR measures fall within tolerance of Isidore (|Hand A − Isidore| <= band). REFUTED if Hand A shows header recurrence >= 0.30 (classical-recipe pattern) OR cross-class >= 0.55 (natural function/content alternation) — either directly contradicts the herbal-encyclopedic reading regardless of benchmark comparison. MARGINAL otherwise (Hand A matches 2-3 of 4 bands with no refute condition). Add GENRE-REFERENCE-UNSTABLE caveat to any verdict if 3 or more measures are form-sensitive (|Isidore − Macer| > 2x tolerance).

tests_run.details: MARGINAL per locked rule. Hand A matches Isidore on 3 of 4 tolerance bands. Full measurements (Hand A | Isidore | Macer | |HA-Iso| | tol | verdict): disjunction 0.849 | 0.771 | 0.333 | 0.078 | 0.10 | MATCH (form-sensitive; Macer verse much lower); top-20 medial 5 | 5 | 15 | 0 | 4 | EXACT MATCH (form-sensitive; Macer verse higher); header recurrence 0.063 | 0.003 | 0.000 | 0.060 | 0.05 | MISS by 0.010; cross-class rate 0.468 | 0.509 | 0.472 | 0.041 | 0.05 | MATCH. No refute conditions triggered (Hand A header 0.063 < 0.30, cross-class 0.468 < 0.55). Unit-size check PASS: Isidore mean paragraph 30.6 tokens vs Hand A 41.1 (within 2x band). Genre-form sensitivity: 2 of 4 measures form-sensitive (disjunction and top-20 medial differ >2x tolerance between Isidore prose and Macer verse); below the 3-of-4 GENRE-REFERENCE-UNSTABLE threshold. Notable: Macer's 31-paragraph segmentation yielded 355-token mean paragraphs (chapter-level), not comparable to Hand A's gallows-bound units; Macer mostly non-diagnostic for paragraph-structure comparison. Substantive reading: Hand A's signature is LARGELY CONSISTENT with Isidore on the three most informative measures (disjunction, cross-class anti-alternation, top-20 medial); the one miss is header recurrence, where Hand A has ~20x more repetition (6.3% vs 0.3%) than Isidore. This single miss is 0.010 above tolerance and suggests Hand A's paragraph-initial tokens are not purely unique topic headers in the Isidore-strict sense — some scribal structural markers repeat across paragraphs. The encyclopedic framework is supported on the vocabulary-stratification and register-clustering axes but refined: Hand A is NOT a perfect encyclopedia; it has occasional repeated section-marker vocabulary that pure Isidore-style encyclopedic prose lacks.

verdict_note: Hand A matches Isidore on 3 of 4 signature bands (disjunction, top-20 medial, cross-class adjacency). The single miss is header recurrence (Hand A 6.3%, Isidore 0.3%, tolerance 5%) — 0.010 above band. The miss is small in absolute terms but interpretable: Isidore's encyclopedic Latin has essentially unique nominal section headers (agricultura, frumentum, granum, ...), whereas Hand A's gallows-delimited paragraph-initial tokens include some repeated scribal markers. The match on the three most informative measures supports the herbal-encyclopedic framework; the header-recurrence miss refines it: Hand A is NOT structurally identical to Isidore, but it is clearly closer to Isidore than to either recipe-manual or natural-language-prose models. Macer verse is non-diagnostic for paragraph structure here because its chapter-level units are 355 tokens mean.
findings_for_followup: The top-20 medial value matched at 5/20 for both Hand A and Isidore under uniform ASCII-vowel-strip stemming, but the prior RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 Hand A measurement was 19/20 using Brady's EVA-based skeleton. This IS an inconsistency worth reconciling: the two stemming functions produce different variant counts. Document which skeleton function is appropriate for each corpus and re-run if the choice is material.

Hand A's 6.3% vs Isidore's 0.3% header recurrence: investigate whether Hand A's repeated paragraph-initial tokens cluster on specific gallows forms (the tcho/tshor/pchor family observed in RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01), or whether they carry semantic load. A gallows-vs-nominal separation of paragraph-initial vocabulary would decide the question.

Find a second medieval Latin prose encyclopedic text (Isidore Etym Book 17 is book-17-length only ~10k tokens — narrow) to confirm the cross-class 0.509 and disjunction 0.771 values are genre-typical rather than Isidore-specific. Candidates: Hrabanus Maurus' De universo, or Vincent of Beauvais' Speculum Naturale excerpts.
amendment_note: Before execution, amended to address four pre-execution concerns: (1) Macer is verse not prose, potentially confounding genre with structure — added Isidore Etymologiae Book 17 as primary PROSE benchmark; Macer relegated to secondary verse-control. (2) Absolute thresholds risked false confirm/refute against a benchmark whose properties were unknown — replaced with relative-match tolerance bands. (3) Paragraph segmentation defined per-corpus (Hand A gallows / Isidore section-numbering / Macer chapter-heading) with unit-size caveat. (4) Reference-anchor asymmetry: a framework test should anchor on the KNOWN genre exemplar, not on the UNKNOWN being characterised. Tolerance bands reframed to centre on ISIDORE (the herbal-encyclopedic reference); Hand A is tested for INCLUSION in those bands. Added genre-form sensitivity flagging via |Isidore − Macer| comparison so that measures where prose and verse disagree are transparently annotated. Commit of this amendment is the new lock point.
reasoning: The RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 result pointed at a herbal-encyclopedic structure. To test that reading rigorously, compare Hand A against a prose medieval Latin encyclopedia (Isidore Book 17), not just a verse didactic poem (Macer). The pre-execution review raised three concerns — verse/prose confound, absolute vs relative thresholds, paragraph-unit mismatch — all three addressed in this amendment.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-directed benchmark test; amended after three pre-execution review concerns
